note: csv_serialization is a passive API module: it registers a `csv` serialization format/encoder with no admin UI or config of its own. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, I have `$data` (an array of associative rows) and want to turn it into a CSV string. Which core service do I call and what format string do I pass? Show the one-line call.
evals.expected_output: Use the core `serializer` service: \Drupal::service('serializer')->serialize($data, 'csv', $context) — the module registers `csv` as a serialization format.

evals.prompt: What is the encoder class this module provides for CSV, and what serialization format string does it support? Name the class and the format.
evals.expected_output: The Drupal\csv_serialization\Encoder\CsvEncoder class (service csv_serialization.encoder.csv, tagged as an `encoder`) supporting the `csv` format.

evals.prompt: I want a Views display that lets users download the results as a downloadable .csv file. Which Views display type / mechanism uses this module to emit CSV, and how is the format selected over REST?
evals.expected_output: A Views "Data export" (Rest export) display outputs CSV via this module; a REST resource returns CSV with ?_format=csv (text/csv).

evals.prompt: This module fails because an external library is missing. Which Composer library does csv_serialization depend on to build and parse CSV, and how do I install it?
evals.expected_output: It requires the league/csv library (^9.16), pulled in via Composer (drupal/csv_serialization requires league/csv).

evals.prompt: In custom PHP I want to serialize an array to CSV but with a semicolon (;) field separator and a single-quote enclosure instead of the defaults. How do I pass those options to the serializer?
evals.expected_output: Pass a $context['csv_settings'] array with 'delimiter' => ';' and 'enclosure' => "'" to serialize().

evals.prompt: My CSV export opens in Excel with garbled accented characters. Which csv_settings keys make the module prepend a UTF-8 BOM so Excel reads it correctly?
evals.expected_output: Set encoding to 'utf8' and utf8_bom to a truthy value in csv_settings so a UTF-8 BOM is prepended.

evals.prompt: When I export multi-value fields to CSV with this module, how are the multiple values combined inside a single cell, and is that separator configurable?
evals.expected_output: Multi-value/nested cells are flattened with a hard-coded `|` separator (e.g. a|b|c); it is not configurable. Decoding explodes `|` cells back into arrays.

evals.prompt: For a CSV export I want clean plain-text cells (no HTML) and I want to turn OFF the header row for a headless consumer. Which csv_settings keys control tag stripping and the header row, and what are their defaults?
evals.expected_output: strip_tags (default TRUE) strips HTML from each value; output_header (default TRUE) toggles the header row — set output_header to FALSE to omit it.
